title: Muhammad Born
description: Muhammad is traditionally dated to about 570 CE in Mecca.

title: First Revelation
description: Islamic tradition holds that Muhammad receives the first Quranic revelation in 610.

title: Public Preaching in Mecca
description: Muhammad begins publicly proclaiming his message in Mecca in the early 7th century.

title: Hijra to Medina
description: In 622 Muhammad and his followers migrate from Mecca to Medina, marking year one of the Islamic calendar.

title: Battle of Badr
description: The Battle of Badr in 624 becomes one of the formative victories of the early Muslim community.

title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
description: In 628 a treaty with Meccan opponents becomes a key turning point for the Medinan community.

title: Conquest of Mecca
description: In 630 Muhammad and his followers take Mecca and cleanse the Kaaba of idols.

title: Death of Muhammad
description: Muhammad dies in 632 in Medina.

title: Rashidun Caliphate
description: The first caliphs lead rapid political and religious expansion after Muhammad's death.

title: Quran Codified
description: A standard written recension of the Quran is associated with the caliph Uthman in the mid-7th century.

title: Battle of Karbala
description: The death of Husayn at Karbala in 680 becomes a defining event in Shi'i Islam.

title: Abbasid Revolution
description: In 750 the Abbasids overthrow the Umayyads and found a new caliphal order.

title: Baghdad Founded
description: Baghdad is founded in 762 and becomes one of the leading cities of the Islamic world.

title: Al-Azhar Founded
description: Al-Azhar is founded in Cairo in the 10th century and becomes one of the most important centers of Islamic learning.

title: Sunni Legal Schools Consolidate
description: By the medieval period, the major Sunni schools of law are firmly established.

title: Delhi Sultanate
description: From 1206, the Delhi Sultanate becomes a major center of Islamic rule in South Asia.

title: Ottomans Take Constantinople
description: In 1453 the Ottoman conquest of Constantinople reshapes the eastern Mediterranean and Islamic imperial history.

title: Safavid Iran Adopts Twelver Shi'ism
description: In the early 16th century, the Safavids establish Twelver Shi'ism as the state religion of Iran.

title: Modern Reform Movements
description: In the 18th and 19th centuries, reform and revival movements reshape Islamic thought in many regions.

title: Ottoman Caliphate Abolished
description: In 1924 the Turkish Republic abolishes the Ottoman caliphate.
